title: roemeria polygonoides
intro: Roemeria polygonoides, also known as the May poppy or Corn poppy, is a flowering plant species native to Western Asia and the Middle East. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating a range of health issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as relieving fever, reducing pain and inflammation, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the roots and flowers of Roemeria polygonoides are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
